Title: Natural variants of AGL80.5 and FPA.3 contribute to bud break timing in poplar by controlling auxin biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes bud break and growth initiation
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Auxin biosynthesis via the indole-3-pyruvate (IPyA) pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: TAA1/TAR branch of de novo auxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts L-tryptophan to indole-3-pyruvate in the main de novo auxin biosynthetic route
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key regulatory factor influencing auxin synthesis rate and concentration
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Differentially expressed in vegetative buds before and after bud break between low- and high-latitude accessions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly relevant in vegetative buds; elevated in PtoTAR2.1-OE and reduced in PtoTAR2.1-RNAi lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bud break stage; natural conditions; artificial growth chamber conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases IAA accumulation and advances bud break when overexpressed; RNAi reduces IAA and delays bud break
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; RNAi; IAA quantification; bud break phenotyping; RT-qPCR; DAP-seq integration; Y1H; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; SPR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtoTAR2.1 encodes a TAA1/TAR-like enzyme in the IPyA auxin biosynthesis pathway. 2. PtoAGL80.5 directly binds and activates the PtoTAR2.1 promoter. 3. PtoTAR2.1 overexpression increases IAA and promotes earlier bud break, whereas RNAi has the opposite effect.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering altered bud break timing through auxin biosynthesis, but deployment should consider local adaptation and climate-specific phenology requirements.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Earlier bud break may increase risk of late frost damage; delayed bud break may shorten the growing season.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; DAP-seq; GWAS; genotype-environment association analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Two TAR2 homologs exist in P. tomentosa, but only PtoTAR2.1 promoter was specifically bound by PtoAGL80.5 in validated assays.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes bud break and growth initiation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PtoTAR2.1 expression; HAP1 shows stronger promoter binding and activation than HAP2
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: PtoSOC1; photoperiod during dormancy; long-day and warm environmental cues are proposed upstream influences
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in apex; dynamically expressed during bud break stage without significant haplotype-specific transcript difference
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed or knocked down in transgenic 84K poplar lines
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Photoperiod during dormancy; transfer from short day/cold to long day/warm conditions; natural field conditions at two latitudes; exogenous IAA and l-kynurenine treatments
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes IAA accumulation and advances bud break; RNAi delays bud break
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; genotype-environment association; overexpression; RNAi; field trials; growth chamber assays; DAP-seq; Y1H; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; SPR; RNA-seq; hormone quantification
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome resequencing; GWAS; LFMM/RDA environmental association; RNA-seq; DAP-seq
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Earlier bud break may raise vulnerability to late frost; delayed bud break may reduce use of the growing season.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising allele/haplotype target for climate-resilient phenology breeding and molecular design of region-specific bud break timing.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtoAGL80.5 is a major adaptive regulator associated with bud break timing and dormancy photoperiod. 2. It directly activates the auxin biosynthesis gene PtoTAR2.1, increasing IAA accumulation. 3. HAP1 has stronger activation of PtoTAR2.1 and is associated with earlier bud break than HAP2.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator linking environmental adaptation to IAA biosynthesis and early bud break.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PtoFPA.3 enhances PtoAGL80.5 mRNA stability/protein accumulation posttranscriptionally; no major haplotype-specific transcript abundance difference was detected, indicating posttranscriptional/functional divergence.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes bud break and growth initiation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SPOC domain and RNA recognition motifs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin biosynthesis via posttranscriptional control of upstream regulator
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds PtoAGL80.5 mRNA and elevates PtoAGL80.5 protein accumulation, thereby indirectly up-regulating PtoTAR2.1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed across multiple developmental tissues, especially apex and immature leaves; dynamically expressed during bud break
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed or knocked down in transgenic 84K poplar lines
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Photoperiod during dormancy; growth chamber and field conditions; actinomycin D treatment; exogenous IAA and l-kynurenine treatments
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes IAA accumulation and earlier bud break through posttranscriptional enhancement of PtoAGL80.5
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; genotype-environment association; overexpression; RNAi; field trials; growth chamber assays; immunoblotting; actinomycin D assay; R-EMSA; protoplast cotransformation; RNA-seq; hormone quantification
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome resequencing; GWAS; LFMM/RDA environmental association; RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Earlier bud break may increase late frost risk.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Superior haplotype PtoFPA.3HAP1 is valuable for tuning bud break timing via posttranscriptional enhancement of the auxin-promoting module.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtoFPA.3 is a major adaptive regulator associated with bud break timing and photoperiod. 2. It binds PtoAGL80.5 mRNA and promotes higher PtoAGL80.5 protein accumulation without major transcript-level changes. 3. This increases downstream PtoTAR2.1 expression and IAA accumulation, accelerating bud break.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important positive upstream regulator of the auxin-promoting PtoAGL80.5-PtoTAR2.1 module controlling bud break.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PtoFPA.3HAP1 and HAP2 showed nearly equivalent binding to a given transcript, whereas PtoAGL80.5HAP1 mRNA was the stronger-bound transcript.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes bud break and growth initiation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MADS-box domain with K-box coiled-coil region
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Upstream transcriptional control of auxin-biosynthesis regulatory cascade
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates promoters of PtoAGL80.5 and PtoFPA.3
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Photoperiodic, vernalization, hormone, and flowering-related cues are discussed contextually; direct upstream inputs in this study not fully resolved
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly up-regulated during the bud break process; shows coexpression with PtoAGL80.5 and PtoFPA.3
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed or knocked down in transgenic 84K poplar lines
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bud break developmental transition under growth chamber conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes earlier bud break by activating PtoAGL80.5 and PtoFPA.3
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-luciferase reporter assay; Y1H; overexpression; RNAi; growth chamber phenotyping; RT-qPCR; WGCNA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate upstream switch for coordinated phenology engineering, though broader pleiotropic effects on developmental transitions should be considered.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtoSOC1 directly activates the promoters of PtoAGL80.5 and PtoFPA.3. 2. PtoSOC1 overexpression advances bud break, whereas RNAi delays it. 3. It acts as an upstream regulator of the auxin-associated bud break cascade.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream activator of the PtoFPA.3-PtoAGL80.5-PtoTAR2.1 module that promotes IAA-associated bud break.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulatory gene because the Results section provides direct mechanistic validation linking it to the auxin-regulatory cascade controlling bud break.
